mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and increases the present value of future equity cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 6.0% signals labor market deterioration and weakening consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in greater than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and rising real estate sales indicates robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns with Rank IC greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production index below 100.0 reflects contracting manufacturing output, which reduces corporate profit margins and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances when INDPRO falls below 100.0.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation readings above 235.0 on the CPI index force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.0.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and rising unemployment indicates labor force participation shifts that mask underlying weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and UNRATE both rise MoM
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window for the interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts above 850.0 thousand units signal resilient consumer confidence and construction sector activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive when HOUST exceeds 850.0 based on a t-test with p-value greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high industrial production and low unemployment creates an overheating supply environment that triggers inflation fears, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.
